mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL erodes real earnings expectations and pressures the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL month-over-month increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling three-month window, labor market deterioration triggers earnings recession fears and forced de-risking, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered events over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and industrial production signal a coordinated contraction in aggregate demand and supply that overwhelms any monetary offset, producing sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month declines in RSAFS and INDPRO are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of coincident decline months, or the signal Rank IC drops below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate above 4.0% acts as a persistent drag on leveraged corporate cash flows by increasing borrowing costs and compressing equity risk premiums, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, positive 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 55% of months over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts indicates collapsing residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Two consecutive monthly declines in HOUST are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Falling industrial production combined with a rising unemployment rate confirms a negative output gap is widening, amplifying downside earnings revisions and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of falling INDPRO and rising UNRATE is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered months, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in CPIAUCSL reduces inflation uncertainty and increases the real value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in CPIAUCSL are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered months over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals expanding aggregate income and corporate profit potential, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive quarter-over-quarter changes in GDPC1 are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year rolling window.
